sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Green bar: 71 pixels, boundary shows it ends at col 51. The red cross is at col 51-53. The bar now touches the cross! The boundary is [[9, 28], [9, 51], [10, 50], [11, 51], [11, 28]] - note the middle row (10) only goes to col 50, not 51. This is because the bar has a missing pixel (from my earlier clicks).
The level didn't complete though. So just touching the cross isn't enough. Let me now grow the yellow bar to reach its red cross.
Yellow bar: currently 32 pixels, starts at row 28, ends at row 38 (32/3 ≈ 10.67, so about 11 rows). Red cross at row 51-53. Need to extend from row 38 to row 51 = 13 more rows = 39 more pixels. 39/9 = 4.33, so 4 clicks of +9 = 36, giving 68 pixels. Or 5 clicks = 45, giving 77 pixels.
Let me try 4 clicks first to get to 68, then check if it's close enough.
Actually, let me calculate more carefully. The yellow bar is 3 columns wide (cols 9-11). Current height: 32/3 ≈ 10.67, so about 11 rows. If I add 36 pixels (4 clicks), that's 12 more rows, so total height ≈ 23 rows. Starting at row 28, ending at row 50. The cross is at row 51-53, so I'd be 1-2 rows short.
Let me try 5 clicks to get to 77 pixels. 77/3 ≈ 25.67 rows. Starting at row 28, ending at row 53. That should reach the cross.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Grow yellow bar to reach the red cross
# Current yellow: 32 pixels, ends at row 38
# Need to reach row 51-53
# Target: 71 pixels (same as green bar target)
# 71 - 32 = 39, 39/9 = 4.33
# Try 5 clicks: 32 + 45 = 77
action([
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 44, 'col': 24},
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 44, 'col': 24},
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 44, 'col': 24},
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 44, 'col': 24},
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 44, 'col': 24},
])

seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] == 'N' and n['pixels'] >= 10:
        print(f"Green bar: pixels={n['pixels']}")
    if n['color'] == 'Y' and n['pixels'] >= 10:
        print(f"Yellow bar: pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
